posts.body:
the rules say "This action is only possible if there are at least 4 tokens of the chosen color left when the player takes them. I have already been taught two different ways. One review said this means you cannot take 2 if there are 4 in the start because the left means after you take them. Another player told me you can take them as long as there are 4 left when you take them. 

Please help: Which is correct?
It means 4 left before you take them. It's clearer in some of the other languages and that's also how the publisher describes it in a video on Tric Trac.

Thanks for the quick reply. A Rules editor of mine told me the word "Left" means that it means you cannot take them if less than 4 are left after your take. I thought your answer made more sense so that in a two player game you at least have one shot at a full stack to get 2.

Mikefitz wrote:

Thanks for the quick reply. A Rules editor of mine told me the word "Left" means that it means you cannot take them if less than 4 are left after your take.

"When the player takes them" ≠ "after the player takes them".

"When the player takes them" = "at the moment that the player takes them".

W Eric Martin wrote:

Mikefitz wrote:

Thanks for the quick reply. A Rules editor of mine told me the word "Left" means that it means you cannot take them if less than 4 are left after your take.

"When the player takes them" ≠ "after the player takes them".

"When the player takes them" = "at the moment that the player takes them".

Yeah, right !

Regards,

CROC

If you couldn't take two gems of a color unless there will be 4 left after you take them, then this option would be available only in the 4-player game!

With 2-3 players, you start with only 4-5 games per color - taking 2 of those would always leave less than 4!

gillum wrote:

If you couldn't take two gems of a color unless there will be 4 left after you take them, then this option would be available only in the 4-player game!

With 2-3 players, you start with only 4-5 games per color - taking 2 of those would always leave less than 4!

So that means, in a 2p game if there are 4 gems/tokens of a certain color, it is allowed to take 2 gems/tokens of that color. In other words: The pair-picking action is allowed. Then 2 tokens of that color are left on the table. The pair-picking action is not allowed anymore (for that color), until at least 4 tokens of that color come back to the table (through payments).
